justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Event sourcing correctly identified as appropriate but not the only option — MJ explicitly presents bi-temporal tables and append-only audit log as alternatives. (2) Schema evolution risk identified and thoroughly explained — called out as high severity with upcasting, versioning, and schema registry mitigations. (3) Snapshotting requirement identified — dedicated Snapshot Service component with rationale for long-lived aggregates. (4) Simpler audit-log alternative presented — both append-only audit log with triggers and bi-temporal tables discussed as alternatives_considered. (5) trade_offs includes substantial gains and costs for each decision. (6) implementation_guidance distinguishes approaches via phased strategy and explicitly notes the simpler alternative may suffice at 30% complexity cost.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough alternatives_considered section listing three distinct simpler approaches (bi-temporal tables, append-only audit log with triggers, CDC via Debezium) with clear trade-off analysis for each
Schema evolution identified as high-severity risk with concrete mitigation strategy including event upcasting, versioning, and schema registry
Snapshotting covered both as a dedicated architectural component and as a risk mitigation with specific guidance (every 50 events, <50ms load target)
GDPR/crypto-shredding risk identified proactively — goes beyond the rubric requirements and shows deep domain awareness for financial services
Honest low_confidence_areas acknowledgment that simpler bi-temporal approach might satisfy compliance at 30% of the complexity cost
Team experience risk explicitly called out as high severity — matches the rubric's concern about presenting ES as purely beneficial
Output is extremely verbose — the implementation_guidance section could be more concise while still distinguishing the two approaches
The executive_summary recommends a hybrid approach as primary, which slightly buries the simpler alternative — could be more neutral in framing the choice

agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"Event sourcing is architecturally well-suited for this loan application audit requirement — the compliance needs (immutable history, point-in-time reconstruction, field-level change tracking) are precisely what event sourcing delivers by design. However, event sourcing introduces significant operational and developmental complexity (event versioning, schema evolution, CQRS projections, eventual consistency) that must be weighed against simpler alternatives. My recommendatio
